summary: Yegge explains two major AI developments from a single week: Geoffrey Hinton warning about LLMs' ability to share knowledge instantly, and a leaked Google memo revealing that open-source LLMs can now achieve near-GPT performance for around $100-300 in training costs. Meta's LLaMA architecture, after its weights leaked, became the foundation for rapid OSS advancement through techniques like LoRA (Low Rank Adaptation), which allows models to learn from each other. This represents a 10-orders-of-magnitude cost reduction in just 10 weeks, effectively commoditizing what was supposed to be a billion-dollar moat for companies like OpenAI and Google.
key_points: LLMs can now share knowledge instantly through techniques like LoRA, making them composable and allowing rapid knowledge transfer between models
Training costs dropped from ~$1 billion to ~$100-300 in roughly 10 weeks, representing 20 years of Moore's Law progress compressed into weeks
Meta accidentally won the AI race by open-sourcing LLaMA, whose architecture became the standard for OSS development after weights leaked
The 'LLM-as-moat' business model is collapsing as AI becomes commoditized; companies now need data moats instead
The Transformer architecture has emergent properties that make it resemble biological brains, with capabilities triggered by simple prompts like 'Let's think step by step'
money_quotes: As Dr. Hinton observes, we have discovered immortality, it's just not for us.
We are now moving over 100 times faster along the exponential technology curve than we were just 15–20 years ago.

Remember when it was roughly $1B to train an LLM like GPT-4? According to the leaked Google memo, world-class competitive LLM training costs just dropped from a billion dollars to… that's right, you guessed it… A hundred dollars.
It's sort of like the decades-long gradual miniaturization of computers from mainframes to embedded chips that run full OSes. Except it happened in ten weeks.
If a big one gets loose, it spreads like wildfire. Well guess what? It's now spreading like wildfire. They are learning from each other now.
Meta, according to Sernau's reasoning, came out the clear winner among the Big Fish. The man has the luck of a Jar Jar Binks. And the everything else, too.
Microsoft and OpenAI will have to go back to regular toilet paper.
key_insight: The LLM moat that companies spent billions building has evaporated in weeks because open-source models can now learn from each other and achieve comparable performance at a fraction of the cost.
